calendar.matters:
Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreements
Off Calendar Jun-16-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Schwartz for and with (Trustee) Mehran Goldchehren. Michael Lamay, Guardian ad Litem.

The Court asked the parties why they are here and not in the civil court. Mr. Schwartz stated that when they settled the case it was very contentious, it is a large family and because it involved a trust they felt that it should come to probate. The Court stated that if there is a pending civil case probate is abated, that there is nothing indicating that this petition should be in probate, that the internal aspects of the trust are not in question and that if it is to be heard in probate they have not satisfied the notice requirements for a trust matter. Mr. Schwartz stated that everyone received notice, that the examiner asked for a proposed order only and that the matter has been continued 3 times but they were never asked for a notice. Mr. Schwartz further stated that this petition was filed in January and that they have given notice to everyone. Mr. Lamay stated that all of the beneficiaries are aware of the petition. The court stated that it cannot do anything today, that there is not proper notice, that because this is a civil case it does not belong in probate, that it cannot proceed without all of the necessary procedural steps regarding a trust matter met and suggested that probate is not the proper place to proceed. The petition for approval of settlement agreements was taken off calendar by the court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
